events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose. Image shows evacuation decay test pump isolated: t0=425 microns, t10=1495 microns. 10-minute rise 1070 microns. That's significant decay, indicates leak or outgassing/moisture. In refrigeration evacuation, acceptable decay typically <500 microns and rise not more than 500 microns over time (often 100-200 microns in 10 min). 1495 microns after 10 min fail. Likely leak in system, possibly moisture. Need decision fail, risk high? Non-compliance with evacuation specs. Component condition non-compliant. value = 1495 (10-minute reading). percent_complete maybe 70? Work_stage tested/inspected. Findings: excessive micron rise during standing decay test indicating leak/moisture/contamination. Actions: locate and repair leak, re-evacuate, re-test; ensure cores removed, hoses tight; replace drier if moisture. References: EPA 608 (proper evacuation), mfr specs. Need include all fields. sound_source etc not asked but include defaults? Schema says use for audio/video tasks when requested; can set empty string/null? JSON shape includes them. We can set sound_source: "", event_time:0.0, rate:0.0, order:[], workable:[]. But maybe rate could be micron rise per minute: (1495-425)/10 = 107.0 microns/min. The task says use rate when requested; not requested. But can include computed rate? Might be okay. event_time 0.0. order empty. workable empty. Need ensure valid JSON. Let's craft.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":false,"risk":"high","work_stage":"tested/inspected","component_conditions":["non-compliant"],"percent_complete":75,"value":1495,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0.0,"rate":107.0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Standing decay test rose from 425 microns to 1495 microns in 10 minutes after pump isolation","Micron level far exceeds acceptable evacuation hold criteria indicating a leak, contamination, or outgassing"],"actions":["Perform leak detection on all joints, valves, and components","Repair any leaks found and replace liquid-line/suction driers as needed","Re-evacuate the system to manufacturer-specified microns and repeat standing decay test","Verify core removal, hose seals, and gauge calibration before retest"],"rationale":"A rise of over 1000 microns in 10 minutes during a standing decay test is unacceptable; the system cannot hold a deep vacuum, so it fails evacuation acceptance criteria and must not be charged until corrected.","references":["EPA 608: required evacuation levels and leak repair before refrigerant charging","OEM service specifications: standing decay test acceptance limits"]}
